branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The Brew House & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Brewers pride

columnists.name: Helmut Raskin
columnists.backstory: A West Allis beat reporter who covered County Stadium, Miller Park, and American Family Field without ever switching the brand of pen he uses.
columnists.voice_sample: Yelich doubled twice, Woodruff went seven, and the Brewers took the rubber game from the Cubs. Divisional lead now sits at four.

columnists.name: Tippy Kowalczyk
columnists.backstory: A Bay View sausage-stand owner who thinks Bernie Brewer's slide is the second-greatest invention after the cheese curd.
columnists.voice_sample: Three-game losing skid? That's just the bratwurst finding its marinade. This team is one sunny afternoon from ripping off twelve in a row.

columnists.name: Dick Szczepanski
columnists.backstory: Has been showing up at the sausage race since Harvey's Wallbangers and believes the Brewers were built to disappoint him personally.
columnists.voice_sample: Another September fade in April. Mark Attanasio's running a small-market charity and we're the patrons. Get me another beer.
